proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs by ecog_ps level (worse PFS with higher ECOG performance status).
Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0.
Patients with right_sided_primary=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided).

analyses.result_summary: PFS decreases by approximately 1.19 months per 1-unit increase in ECOG performance status. Strongly significant.
Stage IV patients have mean PFS approximately 1.35 months shorter than non-stage-IV patients. Strongly significant.
Right-sided primary tumors have mean PFS approximately 0.31 months shorter than left-sided. p ~ 5e-46.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher serum albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive coefficient).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative coefficient).
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative coefficient).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative coefficient).

analyses.result_summary: Each 1 g/dL increase in albumin is associated with a 0.47-month increase in PFS. Strongly significant.
Each 1% increase in 6-month weight loss is associated with a 0.073-month decrease in PFS. Strongly significant.
Each 1 ng/mL increase in CEA is associated with a 0.0053-month decrease in PFS. Significant.
Each 1 U/L increase in LDH is associated with a 0.0003-month decrease in PFS. Marginal significance (p=0.013).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (expected direction in cancer).
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (inflammation hurts).
Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.
sex_female is associated with different mean pfs_months than males.

analyses.result_summary: Each year of age is associated with a 0.176-month INCREASE in PFS — opposite to the expected direction. Highly significant.
CRP is not significantly associated with PFS univariably (p=0.065).
NLR is not significantly associated with PFS (p=0.29).
Female vs male: mean PFS difference is approximately -0.006 months (p=0.77). No association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with kras_mutation=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than KRAS wild-type.
Patients with braf_v600e=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than BRAF wild-type.
Patients with msi_high=1 have different mean pfs_months than MSS patients.
Patients with her2_amplified=1 have different mean pfs_months than HER2-negative.
Patients with nras_mutation=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than NRAS wild-type.

analyses.result_summary: KRAS-mutated patients have mean PFS approximately 0.33 months shorter than KRAS-WT (p ~ 9e-56).
BRAF V600E patients have mean PFS approximately 0.23 months shorter (p ~ 4e-6).
MSI-high status is not associated with PFS univariably (p=0.68).
HER2 amplification is not associated with PFS univariably (p=0.35).
NRAS-mutated patients have mean PFS approximately 0.22 months LONGER than NRAS-WT (p=0.0002) — opposite to the expected direction; effect not robust to multivariable adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than untreated controls.
treatment_cetuximab=1 is associated with different mean pfs_months than untreated controls.
treatment_bevacizumab=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than untreated controls.
treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than untreated controls.
treatment_encorafenib=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months.
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib-treated patients have mean PFS approximately 0.97 months longer than untreated. Highly significant.
Cetuximab-treated patients have mean PFS approximately 0.04 months shorter — not significant (p=0.089).
Bevacizumab is not significantly associated with PFS (p=0.35).
Pembrolizumab is not significantly associated with PFS (p=0.86).
Encorafenib is not significantly associated with PFS (p=0.87).
Trastuzumab/tucatinib is not significantly associated with PFS (p=0.25).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, stage, sidedness, biomarkers, and lab covariates, regorafenib retains a positive effect on pfs_months.
After adjusting for prognostic covariates, cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib show no overall mean PFS effect.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable model (R^2=0.86) confirms: regorafenib coef = +0.943 (p<1e-300). Cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab/tucatinib all p>0.06. Albumin, age, ECOG, stage IV, KRAS, BRAF, right-sided, weight loss, CEA, LDH all retain strong significant effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of treatment_cetuximab is concentrated in pan-RAS/BRAF wild-type patients (effect ~0 in mutated patients).
The PFS benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab is concentrated in msi_high=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab x KRAS interaction coef=+0.020 (p=0.665), x NRAS=-0.007 (p=0.96), x BRAF=-0.026 (p=0.81). Stratified: in pan-RAS/BRAF-WT (n=25324), cetuximab effect = -0.045 mo (p=0.17). Cetuximab shows NO benefit in any biomarker stratum.
Pembrolizumab x MSI-H interaction coef=+0.002 (p=0.99). Stratified: in MSI-H subgroup (n=2513), pembro effect = +0.007 mo (p=0.96). Pembrolizumab shows NO benefit, even in MSI-H.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of treatment_encorafenib is concentrated in braf_v600e=1 patients.
The PFS benefit of treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is concentrated in her2_amplified=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Encorafenib x BRAF V600E interaction coef=-0.141 (p=0.38). Stratified: in BRAF V600E subgroup (n=2272), encorafenib effect = -0.13 mo (p=0.33). NO benefit for encorafenib in BRAF V600E subgroup.

Trastuzumab/tucatinib x HER2 interaction coef=+0.063 (p=0.78). Stratified: in HER2-amplified subgroup (n=1504), the treatment effect = +0.017 mo (p=0.93). NO benefit even in the biomarker-positive group.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS effect of treatment_regorafenib differs by kras_mutation status — benefit concentrated in KRAS-WT.
The PFS effect of treatment_regorafenib differs by braf_v600e status — benefit concentrated in BRAF-WT.
The PFS effect of treatment_regorafenib differs by right_sided_primary — benefit concentrated in left-sided.

analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib x KRAS interaction coef=-1.629 (p ~ 7.7e-226). Effect of regorafenib: KRAS-WT = +1.66 mo, KRAS-mut = +0.027 mo. KRAS mutation eliminates the regorafenib benefit.
Regorafenib x BRAF V600E interaction coef=-1.183 (p ~ 6e-23). Effect of regorafenib: BRAF-WT = +1.03 mo, BRAF-mut = -0.16 mo. BRAF V600E eliminates (slightly reverses) the benefit.
Regorafenib x right-sided interaction coef=-1.454 (p ~ 4e-168). Effect of regorafenib: left-sided = +1.48 mo, right-sided = +0.026 mo. Right-sided primary eliminates the benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Regorafenib effect is preserved or larger in nras_mutation=1 vs nras_mutation=0 (NRAS does NOT suppress regorafenib benefit).
Regorafenib effect does not differ by msi_high status.
Regorafenib effect does not differ by her2_amplified status.

analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib x NRAS interaction coef=+0.988 (p ~ 1.2e-11). Effect of regorafenib: NRAS-WT = +0.94 mo, NRAS-mut = +1.93 mo. NRAS mutation appears to AUGMENT regorafenib benefit (opposite of KRAS).
Regorafenib x MSI-H interaction coef=-0.022 (p=0.85). Regorafenib effect approximately equal in MSS (+0.97) and MSI-H (+0.95). MSI status not a modifier.
Regorafenib x HER2 interaction coef=-0.107 (p=0.46). HER2 amplification not a significant modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stratified analysis: in KRAS-mutated patients, regorafenib has no PFS benefit.
Stratified analysis: in right-sided primary patients, regorafenib has no PFS benefit.
Stratified analysis: in BRAF V600E patients, regorafenib has no (or negative) PFS benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Among KRAS-mutated patients (n=20898): mean PFS regorafenib=4.07 vs no-rego=4.04, diff=+0.027 mo (p=0.42). No benefit.
Among right-sided patients (n=17657): regorafenib effect approximately +0.03 mo (p>0.1). No benefit.
Among BRAF V600E patients (n=2272): regorafenib effect approximately -0.16 mo (NS).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stratified analysis: in MSI-H patients, regorafenib retains its full benefit (+~0.95 mo).
Stratified analysis: in HER2-amplified patients, regorafenib retains its benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Among MSI-H patients (n=2513): regorafenib mean PFS = 5.18 vs control = 4.23, diff=+0.95 mo (p ~ 7.9e-12). Benefit retained.

analyses.code: Among MSS patients: regorafenib effect = +0.97 mo (p < 1e-200). Benefit identical to overall.
analyses.result_summary: MSS regorafenib effect +0.97 (p ~ 2.7e-206). MSI status does not modify regorafenib effect. (HER2-amp subgroup too small for stable estimate but interaction test was NS.)

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the joint subgroup defined by left-sided AND KRAS-WT AND BRAF-WT, regorafenib produces a much larger PFS benefit than in any other subgroup.
In the complement of (left + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT) — i.e., right-sided OR KRAS-mut OR BRAF-mut — regorafenib has no PFS effect.

analyses.result_summary: Within left + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT (n=17372): mean PFS regorafenib = 6.875 vs control = 4.117, diff = +2.76 mo (p<1e-300). Massive concentrated benefit.

analyses.code: ttest_ind among complement of joint subgroup, by treatment_regorafenib
analyses.result_summary: In complement (right OR KRAS-mut OR BRAF-mut, n=32628): mean PFS regorafenib = 4.147 vs control = 4.117, diff = +0.030 mo (p=0.29). No detectable benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: All three predicates — left-sided primary, KRAS-WT, AND BRAF-WT — are simultaneously required: any one violator alone abolishes the regorafenib benefit.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests in each pure violator subgroup (right + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT; left + KRAS-mut + BRAF-WT; left + KRAS-WT + BRAF-mut)
analyses.result_summary: Right-sided + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT (n=9458): rego effect = +0.083 mo (p=0.12). Left + KRAS-mut + BRAF-WT (n=13490): rego effect = +0.061 mo (p=0.17). Left + KRAS-WT + BRAF-mut (n=1481): rego effect = -0.20 mo (p=0.12). All single-violator subgroups show effectively no benefit. Confirms all three predicates are required.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the regorafenib-benefit subgroup (left + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT), NRAS mutation does not abolish — and may slightly augment — the regorafenib benefit.

analyses.code: Stratified analysis within benefit subgroup, by NRAS
analyses.result_summary: Within benefit subgroup, NRAS-WT (n=16443): rego effect = +2.75 mo (p<1e-300). NRAS-mut (n=929): rego effect = +2.91 mo (p ~ 7e-25). NRAS mutation does not suppress benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Systematic interaction screen: among all binary feature x treatment combinations, the only large interactions are regorafenib x {kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary, nras_mutation}.
Pembrolizumab x right_sided_primary interaction is borderline negative.

analyses.code: Loop over all (treatment, binary_feature) pairs and test interaction
analyses.result_summary: Of 54 treatment x binary-feature interactions tested, only 4 reach p<0.001: regorafenib x kras (p~7e-226, coef=-1.63), regorafenib x right_sided (p~4e-168, coef=-1.45), regorafenib x braf_v600e (p~6e-23, coef=-1.18), regorafenib x nras (p~1e-11, coef=+0.99). Bevacizumab x braf_v600e weak (p=0.022, coef=-0.23). All other treatment-by-biomarker interactions p>0.05.
Pembrolizumab x right_sided interaction coef=-0.116 (p=0.05). Borderline; effect of pembro in right-sided slightly negative, in left-sided ~0. Likely chance given many tests.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous modifier screen: only regorafenib shows a strong continuous-feature interaction; specifically, higher cea_ng_ml attenuates regorafenib benefit.

analyses.code: Loop over all treatment x continuous feature interaction tests
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib x cea_ng_ml interaction coef=-0.025 (p ~ 8.7e-58). All other treatment x continuous interactions p>0.02 or NS after multiple-testing context. Pembrolizumab x crp marginal (p=0.02).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the regorafenib-benefit subgroup (left + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT), higher cea_ng_ml strongly attenuates the regorafenib PFS benefit.
Outside the regorafenib-benefit subgroup, the rego x CEA interaction is absent.

analyses.code: Within benefit subgroup, regress pfs ~ rego + cea + rego*cea
analyses.result_summary: Within benefit subgroup: rego x CEA interaction coef = -0.0649 (p ~ 7e-136). Regorafenib's PFS benefit shrinks by ~0.065 mo per 1 ng/mL CEA — i.e., the benefit is concentrated in low-CEA patients within the responding subgroup.

analyses.code: Outside benefit subgroup: rego x cea
analyses.result_summary: In the complement subgroup, rego x CEA coef = +0.0015 (p=0.41). The CEA modifier is specific to the benefit-receiving subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the regorafenib-benefit subgroup, sex_female augments the regorafenib effect (women benefit slightly more than men).
Within the regorafenib-benefit subgroup, stage_iv augments the regorafenib effect.

analyses.code: Within benefit subgroup, regress pfs ~ rego + sex_female + rego*sex_female
analyses.result_summary: Within benefit subgroup: rego x sex_female interaction coef = +0.314 (p ~ 5e-4). Modest augmentation; effect is small in absolute terms relative to the +2.75-mo main effect.

analyses.code: Within benefit subgroup, regress pfs ~ rego + stage_iv + rego*stage_iv
analyses.result_summary: Within benefit subgroup: rego x stage_iv interaction coef = +0.240 (p ~ 5e-3). Modest augmentation among stage IV patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The regorafenib PFS benefit is robust across ECOG performance status strata (similar magnitude in ECOG 0, 1, 2).
Regorafenib x ECOG interaction is null.

analyses.code: Stratify by ecog_ps level and run t-test for regorafenib effect
analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0: rego diff = +0.92 mo (p ~ 5e-76). ECOG 1: +0.99 mo (p ~ 4e-122). ECOG 2: +0.90 mo (p ~ 3e-34). Effect approximately constant.
Regorafenib x ECOG_ps interaction coef = 0.005 (p=0.89). No interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Three-way interaction model rego x kras_mut x braf_v600e confirms each mutation independently abolishes benefit.
Pure cell counts: in left-sided + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT, the regorafenib effect is approximately +2.76; in any cell with KRAS-mut, BRAF-mut, or right-sided, the effect is between -0.20 and +0.08 (NS).

analyses.code: Multi-way OLS: pfs ~ rego + kras + braf + right + rego*kras + rego*braf + kras*braf + rego*right
analyses.result_summary: rego main effect (in baseline left+kras-WT+braf-WT): +2.33 mo (p<1e-300). rego*kras = -1.80 (p<1e-300), rego*braf = -1.97 (p<1e-300), rego*right = -1.47 (p<1e-300). All three predicates independently and additively suppress benefit.

analyses.code: 8-cell crosstab of {right, kras, braf} with rego effect
analyses.result_summary: Cell (right=0, kras=0, braf=0): rego diff = +2.76 (p~0). All other observed cells have rego diff between -0.20 and +0.08, none significant. Cells (kras=1, braf=1) have 0 patients (probably mutually exclusive in dataset construction).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cetuximab benefit is absent across CRC standard biomarker-defined subgroups (left+pan-WT, left-only, pan-WT-only) — no detectable PFS benefit anywhere.
Pembrolizumab benefit is absent in MSI-H — contrary to the canonical CRC expectation, no PFS benefit detected in this dataset.
Encorafenib benefit is absent in BRAF V600E — no PFS benefit detected.
Trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit is absent in HER2-amplified — no PFS benefit detected.

analyses.code: ttest in mask_left+panWT, also mask_pan-WT, also each violator subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab in left+pan-WT (n=16443): diff = -0.067 mo (p=0.12). In pan-WT regardless of side (n=25324): -0.045 (p=0.17). In any RAS/BRAF mut: -0.034 (p=0.25). Sub-cell results (left+KRAS-mut: -0.036; left+NRAS-mut: -0.114; left+BRAF-mut: -0.026): all NS. No detectable cetuximab benefit anywhere.
Pembrolizumab in MSI-H (n_tx=396, n_ctrl=2117): diff = +0.007 mo (p=0.96). No benefit.

analyses.code: ttest in BRAF V600E, by treatment_encorafenib
analyses.result_summary: Encorafenib in BRAF V600E (n_tx=242, n_ctrl=2030): diff = -0.13 mo (p=0.33). No benefit.
Trastuzumab/tucatinib in HER2-amplified (n_tx=120, n_ctrl=1384): diff = +0.017 mo (p=0.93). No benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cetuximab + bevacizumab combination does not produce a multiplicative effect (interaction null).
Bevacizumab x stage_iv interaction is null — bevacizumab has no main or stage-modified effect.

analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab*bevacizumab interaction coef = -0.055 (p=0.22). No combination effect.
Bev effect in stage IV: -0.019 mo (p=0.48). Bev effect in non-stage-IV: -0.015 mo (p=0.61). No effect in either subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final composite subgroup hypothesis (regorafenib): treatment_regorafenib produces a positive PFS effect ONLY in patients who are simultaneously left-sided primary (right_sided_primary=0), KRAS wild-type (kras_mutation=0), AND BRAF wild-type (braf_v600e=0); within this subgroup the mean PFS gain is approximately +2.76 months. Outside this subgroup the effect is null.
Within the regorafenib benefit subgroup, the effect is further modulated by cea_ng_ml: the benefit decreases by ~0.065 mo per 1 ng/mL CEA, so it is largest in low-CEA patients.

analyses.code: ttest_ind between regorafenib-treated and untreated within (right=0)&(kras=0)&(braf=0); compare to complement
analyses.result_summary: Final subgroup test: in left + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT (n=17372): regorafenib mean PFS = 6.875 vs control 4.117, diff = +2.76 mo (p effectively 0). In complement (n=32628): diff = +0.030 mo (p=0.29). Clean signal.

analyses.code: Within benefit subgroup, regress pfs ~ rego + cea + rego*cea
analyses.result_summary: Within benefit subgroup, rego*cea coef = -0.0649 (p ~ 7e-136). Confirms the regorafenib benefit is concentrated at low CEA values within the responding subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final null treatment hypotheses: cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib show no detectable PFS effect — overall, in any biomarker-defined subgroup, or in any straightforward interaction screen.

Final prognostic summary: in this cohort, the strongest negative prognostic predictors of pfs_months are ecog_ps, stage_iv, kras_mutation, braf_v600e, weight_loss_pct_6mo, right_sided_primary, cea_ng_ml, ldh_u_l; the strongest positive is age_years (counterintuitive) and albumin_g_dl. msi_high and her2_amplified have no main effect on PFS in this dataset.

analyses.code: Compilation of all stratified t-tests and interaction p-values for the five non-regorafenib treatments
analyses.result_summary: Across all main-effect, biomarker-stratified, and interaction tests: cetuximab effects ranged -0.114 to +0.052 mo (all p>0.1); pembrolizumab in MSI-H = +0.007 (p=0.96); encorafenib in BRAF V600E = -0.13 (p=0.33); trastuzumab/tucatinib in HER2 amp = +0.017 (p=0.93); bevacizumab adjusted = +0.003 (p=0.70). Five of the six treatments show no detectable PFS benefit.

Adjusted (R^2=0.86): age_years +0.176 (p<1e-300), albumin_g_dl +0.469 (p<1e-300), ecog_ps -1.171 (p<1e-300), stage_iv -1.362 (p<1e-300), kras_mutation -0.354 (p<1e-300), braf_v600e -0.357 (p~4e-79), right_sided_primary -0.287 (p~9e-273), weight_loss_pct_6mo -0.076 (p<1e-300), cea_ng_ml -0.005 (p~2e-97), ldh_u_l -0.0004 (p~7e-19). msi_high p=0.65, her2_amplified p=0.48, ntrk_fusion p=0.47, sex_female p=0.19 — all NS in adjusted model.
